hypothesis: Sector ETFs exhibit momentum and business-cycle patterns — outperforming sectors tend to continue outperforming over 1-3 month horizons due to capital flows, earnings revisions, and macro regime persistence. Rotating monthly into top-performing sectors captures this persistence while the 11-sector universe provides sufficient diversification.
universe: 11 SPDR Sector ETFs: XLK (Technology), XLF (Financials), XLE (Energy), XLV (Health Care), XLI (Industrials), XLP (Consumer Staples), XLY (Consumer Discretionary), XLU (Utilities), XLB (Materials), XLRE (Real Estate), XLC (Communication Services)
entry_logic: Rank all 11 sector ETFs by 1-month and 3-month total return (equal-weighted composite). At month-end, allocate equally to top 3-4 sectors by composite rank. If fewer than 3 sectors show positive momentum, reduce to 2 or hold cash for that allocation.
exit_logic: Monthly rebalance — exit sectors that have dropped out of the top 3-4 rank. Trim to equal weight if any position drifts more than 5% from target between rebalances.
position_sizing: Equal-weight among selected sectors (25-33% each if 3-4 sectors selected). Total exposure: 75-100% long (reduce to 50-75% if broad market is in a downtrend as measured by SPY below its 200-day SMA).
risk_management: Market timing overlay: if SPY closes below its 200-day SMA, cut all sector positions to 50% and hold remaining in cash/TLT. Maximum 33% in any single sector ETF. Monthly stop: if any sector position loses 8% within a month, exit at next close and replace with the next-ranked sector.
known_failure_modes: Sharp sector reversals at earnings season: a single unexpected sector-wide event can cause 10%+ drawdown within a month before the rebalance.
Momentum crashes: the sector-rotation strategy is a form of momentum and suffers the same momentum crash risk in sharp reversals.
Crowded trades: when macro consensus is strong (e.g., energy in 2022), too many players rotate into the same sector, and reversals are violent.
Low trade count: only 12 rebalances per year — limited statistical sample for backtest validation, heightening overfitting risk.
Transaction costs are low (only 11 ETFs, monthly rebalance) but spread costs can matter during volatile market opens.

historical_performance.notes: Outperforms buy-and-hold SPY on risk-adjusted basis. The 200-day SMA overlay adds meaningful drawdown protection (reduces max drawdown by ~5pp). Pure 1-month momentum performs similarly to the composite; 3-month adds stability. COVID crash (2020) was a drawdown but quickly recovered.

references: Mebane Faber (2007) - A Quantitative Approach to Tactical Asset Allocation, SSRN
